stocks.impact_theory: Tesla reported Q1 2026 earnings beating EPS estimates (0.41 vs 0.37) and revenue ($22.4B), with improving margins from services/FSD. However, high capex ($25B planned), inventory buildup, and cautious guidance led to post-earnings slip. Economic implications: EV demand softening amid high rates; market psychology: disappointment despite beat due to lofty valuations (PE 100+). Historical parallels: Similar post-earnings dips in 2024/2025 followed by recoveries within 60 days. Analogies: 1. High-jumper clearing bar but crowd expecting moonshot. 2. Fireworks fizzling after bright start.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: X sentiment mixed/neutral post-earnings; initial 4% AH pop faded to -3% today. Web news highlights beats but capex/margin concerns; high expectations priced in perfection.

potential_profit_risks.long_position: Buy dip for 10-15% recovery in 1-2 months on historical pattern.
potential_profit_risks.short_position: Short for 5-8% near-term on momentum fade.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Elon comments/Musk factor reversal; broader tech selloff or positive FSD/Optimus updates.

stocks.justification: Strong historical recovery pattern but current macro (oil/geopolitics hurting growth stocks) adds uncertainty.

stocks.impact_theory: Oil prices surged >$100/bbl on stalled US-Iran talks, Hormuz impasse/blockade fears disrupting 20% global supply. Implications: Higher energy costs boost integrated majors' refining margins; psychology: Risk-on for energy amid stagflation fears. Parallels: 2019 Iran tensions, 2022 Ukraine oil spike. Analogies: 1. Tailwind filling sails of oil tanker. 2. Drought benefiting well owners.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Web/X highlight oil rise despite ceasefire; energy outperforming S&P. Sentiment shift positive on supply risks.

potential_profit_risks.short_position: Avoid; limited downside unless quick de-escalation.
potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Sudden Hormuz reopening or ceasefire breakthrough crashing oil.

stocks.justification: Clear supply disruption link to oil prices; historical geo-premiums reliable.

stocks.impact_theory: Similar to XOM; Chevron benefits from upstream production and downstream refining amid oil spike from Hormuz tensions. Implications: Windfall profits, dividend appeal in uncertain markets. Psychology: Flight to energy stability. Parallels: Gulf War oil shocks. Analogies: 1. Rainmaker in desert economy. 2. Anchor holding firm in storm.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Tied to oil surge; X mentions energy trouncing market.

stocks.impact_theory: Rising oil/jet fuel from Hormuz squeezes airline margins (fuel ~30% costs). Implications: Higher fares, demand destruction risk. Psychology: Risk-off for transports. Parallels: 2022 oil spike hurting carriers. Analogies: 1. Headwind battering plane. 2. Fuel guzzler in gas crisis.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: S&P losers include airlines; oil up hurts sector.

stocks.justification: Strong inverse oil-airline link, but hedges vary.

stocks.impact_theory: Oil services ramp activity on higher prices/investment. Hormuz boosts E&P spending. Implications: Backlog growth. Psychology: Levered energy play. Parallels: Post-2021 oil recovery. Analogies: 1. Toolbox for oil boom. 2. Pickaxe in gold rush.

news_impact_analysis.reasoning: Oil up drives services; sector outperforming.

potential_profit_risks.key_risks: Oil capex lag; recession curbs drilling.

stocks.justification: Lagged response to oil but strong fundamentals.
